recalls.Description: Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These containers, which feature a red body with black caps and yellow spouts, have dual chambers that hold 6 liters of gasoline on one side and 2.5 liters of oil on the other. They are marked with the names "Scepter" and "Essence Danger" on the side. The design fails to meet essential safety standards, increasing the risk of flash fires and posing potential poisoning hazards to children due to non-child-resistant features.
recalls.Title: Recall of Scepter Gas and Oil Fuel Containers Due to Fire Hazard and Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Mameix Retail at 587-816-2804 between 11 a.m. and 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email scepterrecall@gmail.com.

Products.Name: Scepter Gas and Oil Combination Fuel Containers

Images.Caption: Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front
Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase on Amazon.com from August 2024 to May 2025, priced around $40.

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire and child poisoning due to non-compliant safety features.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the containers immediately, keep them away from children, and request a full refund from Mameix Retail.

recalls.metaDescription: Scepter fuel containers recalled for fire hazards and child safety risks. Contact Mameix Retail for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Astemrey is recalling its Y'all Can DIY bioethanol fireplace fuel containers due to a serious risk of flash fire. These fuel containers are clear bottles with black tops and feature a label that includes the brand and product name in white and orange, along with phrases like 'No Soot', 'No Ash', and 'Clean Burning'. Hazard warnings are also printed on the side of the bottles. The recall affects products that do not meet safety standards for portable fuel containers, lacking essential flame mitigation devices.
recalls.Title: Astemrey Recalls Y'all Can DIY Bioethanol Fireplace Fuel Containers Due to Fire Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Astemrey via email at contact@astemrey.com.

Products.Name: Y'all Can DIY Bioethanol Fireplace Fuel Containers

Distributors.Name: STARKIM KIMYA SAN.TIC. A.S, of Turkey

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire due to lack of required flame mitigation devices.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled fuel, label it 'DO NOT USE', and contact Astemrey for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Astemrey recalls Y'all Can DIY bioethanol fireplace fuel due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description:
Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are being recalled due to serious safety hazards. These containers, which feature a red body with black caps and yellow spouts, have a dual-chamber design with a 6-liter capacity for gasoline and a 2.5-liter capacity for oil. The containers are marked with "Scepter" and "Gasoline Danger" on the side, along with a Fire Marshall stamp located near the fuel spout. The lack of required safety features poses a risk of flash fires and potential harm to children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these recalled containers immediately and keep them out of children's reach. Contact Canada Prep and Ship for a full refund and follow the instructions to mark the container as recalled and dispose of it properly.
recalls.Title: Recall of Scepter Gas and Oil Combination Fuel Containers Due to Fire and Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, reach out to Canada Prep via email at info@canadaprepandship.com, or visit https://canadaprepandship.com/recalls or www.canadaprepandship.com and select the "Recall" option at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Scepter Gas and Oil Combination Fuel Containers

Images.Caption: Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front
Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – side

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from December 2024 through August 2025 for approximately $36.

Importers.Name: Lowe Opco Inc., dba, Canada Prep and Ship, of Canada

Hazards.Name: These fuel containers do not meet mandatory safety standards, lacking flame mitigation devices, which increases the risk of flash fires. Additionally, the spout is not child-resistant, posing burn and poisoning hazards to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the containers, keep them away from children, and contact Canada Prep and Ship for a full refund. They should also label the container as "Recalled" with a permanent marker and send a photo of it in the trash to info@canadaprepandship.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Scepter recalls gas and oil fuel containers due to fire hazards and child safety risks. Learn how to get a refund and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description: Scepter is recalling its B62 gas and oil fuel containers due to serious safety risks. These containers are red with black caps and yellow spouts, featuring dual chambers with a 6-liter capacity for gasoline and a 2.5-liter capacity for oil. The words 'Scepter' and 'Essence Danger' are embossed on the side, indicating their design. The containers do not meet safety standards, lacking necessary flame mitigation devices and child-resistant closures, which heightens the risk of flash fires and potential poisoning for children. Consumers are urged to stop using these containers immediately.
recalls.Title: Scepter Gas and Oil Fuel Containers Recalled Due to Fire and Poisoning Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Pro Recycle at 514-887-7309 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email prorecycle@hotmail.ca.

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire and child poisoning due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the containers, keep them away from children, and return for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Scepter recalls gas and oil fuel containers due to fire and poisoning risks. Stop using immediately and contact Pro Recycle for a refund.

recalls.Description: Firefly Fuel is recalling its Safe & Green 32 oz. fuel bottles due to a serious risk of poisoning in young children. The bottles are clear with a white cap and nozzle applicator, featuring the 'Firefly' brand and 'Safe & Green' prominently displayed. The labeling claims the product is 'Non-Toxic' and 'Readily Biodegradable', but it fails to meet mandatory child-resistant packaging standards, posing a significant hazard.
recalls.Title: Firefly Fuel Recalls Safe & Green 32 oz. Fuel Bottles Due to Poisoning Risk for Young Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Firefly Fuel Inc. at 888-472-6740 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email support@fireflyfuel.com, or visit www.fireflyfuel.com and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Products.Name: Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. Fuel Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. fuel bottles
Recalled Firefly Kosher Safe & Green 32 oz. bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Firefly Fuels Inc., of Sarasota, Florida

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and www.fireflyfuel.com from August 2019 through May 2025 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning to young children due to non-child-resistant packaging and misleading labeling.

Remedies.Name: Remove the nozzle applicator and replace it with the included child-resistant closure. If unavailable, keep the bottle out of children's reach and contact Firefly Fuel for a free corrected label and replacement lid.

recalls.metaDescription: Firefly Fuel recalls Safe & Green 32 oz. bottles due to poisoning risks for children. Learn how to secure your product safely.

recalls.Description: Jonas C is recalling its Long-Haul 5L ATV gas cans, which are marked with "LONG-HAUL," "TZYC," and various fuel container warnings. These containers, which are black and come with multiple spout components, fail to meet child-resistant closure requirements, posing significant risks of burns, fires, and poisoning, especially to children. The recall is a response to violations of the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act.
recalls.Title: Jonas C Recalls Long-Haul 5L ATV Fuel Containers Due to Serious Burn and Poisoning Risks

Images.Caption: Recalled Long-Haul Portable Fuel Container with Accessories

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from January 2023 to October 2024 for approximately $50.

Distributors.Name: Cheng du shi ri teng mao yi you xian gong si, DBA Jonas C, of London, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant child-resistant closures leading to burn, fire, and poisoning hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled containers immediately, keep them away from children, and reach out to Jonas C for instructions on returning or disposing of the product for a full or partial refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall of Long-Haul 5L ATV fuel containers due to severe burn and poisoning hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Randder has issued a recall for its 750mL and 1500mL Liquid Fuel Bottles due to safety concerns. These bottles, which are red with black caps, feature markings such as "RANDDER", "fuel bottle", and various warnings. The products fail to meet child-resistant closure standards mandated by the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act, increasing the risk of burns and poisoning for children. Consumers are advised to take immediate action regarding these items.
recalls.Title: Randder Liquid Fuel Bottles Recalled for Burn and Poisoning Risks; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email catherine0216@outlook.com or visit https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A109LSFCBRYVBT&asin=B08TCG5L2J&ref_=dp_merchant_link&isAmazonFulfilled=1.

Images.Caption: Recalled Randder 2-Pack Liquid Fuel Bottles – 750mL

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from September 2022 to November 2023, priced at approximately $26 for the 750mL size and $47 for the 1500mL size.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshi Rande Keji Youxian Gongsi, dba Render Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with child-resistant closure requirements, posing burn and poisoning risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles, keep them away from children, and contact Render Store for a child-resistant cap.

recalls.metaDescription: Randder recalls Liquid Fuel Bottles due to safety risks for children. Learn how to get a replacement cap.

recalls.Description: BRS portable liquid fuel bottles are being recalled due to safety concerns related to poisoning and burn risks. These bottles are orange with a black cap and are available in sizes of 530mL, 750mL, and 1000mL. Each bottle features white lettering indicating the brand, volume, and safety warnings. The products were sold exclusively on Amazon.com.
recalls.Title: Recall of BRS Liquid Fuel Bottles Due to Poisoning and Burn Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please email chencaiyu1994@hotmail.com or visit the Amazon seller profile for Huenco.

recalls.Description:
BRS and BULin portable liquid fuel bottles are being recalled due to safety concerns. The BRS bottle is orange with a black cap, while the BULin bottle is red with a matching red cap. Both products were available exclusively in a 1000mL size and feature text in Chinese, including the brand names and safety warnings in white lettering. These bottles do not meet child-resistant closure requirements, increasing the risk of burns and poisoning for children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these fuel bottles immediately and keep them out of children's reach. Tentock is providing instructions for safe disposal and will issue full refunds for the recalled items.
recalls.Title: Recall of BRS and BULin Liquid Fuel Bottles Due to Burn and Poisoning Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, email squarebird@aliyun.com or visit their Amazon seller profile at https://www.amazon.com/s?me=A1778LF2VCKZ0J&marketplaceID=ATVPDKIKX0DER.

Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle – back
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle –front
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle – back

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from January 2023 to December 2023, priced between $10 and $13.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with child-resistant closure requirements, posing burn and poisoning risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles, keep them away from children, and contact Tentock for disposal instructions and a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: BRS and BULin liquid fuel bottles recalled due to burn and poisoning risks. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Worthington Enterprises has issued a recall for EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinders identified by model number EVAS - M0859. These cylinders feature a serial number that starts with 'W' and a date code of either '06-23' or '07-23,' which can be found stamped on the cylinder's collar. Initially sold under the AmeriGas label, these cylinders may have been exchanged at various propane distributors, who applied their own branding.
recalls.Title: Recall of EVAS 20 lb. Propane Exchange Tanks by Worthington Enterprises Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Worthington at 888-520-1304, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, or email propanecylinders@realtimeresults.net. More information can be found at https://www.recallrtr.com/PropaneCylinders.

Images.Caption: Recalled EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinder
Location of model number of recalled EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinder
Scan QR Code for propane exchange retail locations

Retailers.Name: AmeriGas propane cylinder exchange suppliers in New Hampshire and the Boston Metropolitan area of Eastern Massachusetts from July 2023 to August 2023, or from any propane cylinder exchange supplier after.

Distributors.Name: AmeriGas, of King of Prussia, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Potential gas leak creating a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease using the recalled propane cylinders, securely close the top valve, and return them to any AmeriGas Propane Exchange location for a complimentary replacement. For locations, visit https://www.amerigas.com/locations/find-propane. Ensure the valve is completely closed when returning.

recalls.metaDescription: Worthington recalls EVAS 20 lb. propane exchange tanks due to fire hazards. Learn how to return and replace your cylinder safely.

recalls.Description: WAOLi is recalling its BRS and BULin portable liquid fuel bottles, which were produced between February and July 2023. The BRS bottle is orange with a black cap and comes in 500mL, 530mL, and 750mL sizes, while the BULin bottle is red with a red cap and is available in a 1500mL size. Each bottle features the brand name, volume, warnings, and directions in white lettering.
recalls.Title: Recall of WAOLi BRS and BULin Liquid Fuel Bottles Due to Poisoning and Fire Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, email lyuxia3k@163.com or visit WAOLi's seller profile on Amazon.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle - front
Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle - back
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle - front
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle - back

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from February 2023 to November 2023, priced between $12 and $40.

Hazards.Name: The fuel bottles do not comply with child-resistant closure requirements, posing burn and poisoning risks to children. The 530mL BRS bottle lacks a flame mitigation device, creating a flash fire risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled bottles immediately and keep them away from children. To obtain a full refund, WAOLi requests that consumers initial and date the bottles, then send a photo of them in the trash along with their refund request.

recalls.metaDescription: WAOLi recalls BRS and BULin liquid fuel bottles due to serious burn and poisoning hazards. Immediate action required for refunds.

Manufactured prior to July 2023, these orange bottles feature a black cap and white text indicating the brand and volume. recalls.Title: Recall of BRS Fuel Bottles Due to Burn and Poisoning Risks; Non-Compliant with Child Safety Standards
